structure.rationale: Premium tariff (7+ pages): editorial long-form home as a self-contained narrative + dedicated /how-it-works (5 phases deep dive), /pricing (honest spec-sheet), /case-studies (list) + /case-study/[slug] (detail template), /about (team + AI-pipeline-as-member), /contacts, /404. Multi-page separate (not single-responsive long-scroll) preserves editorial pacing and matches the explicit brief request for separate mobile-flow on premium.

sections.name: Hero
sections.purpose: Open with the core promise — site in 24 hours on our AI-pipeline, not a template — and the primary CTA (request demo).

sections.name: Что мы делаем
sections.purpose: Three tariffs explained in plain language (Landing / Multipage / Premium) with timeline and price upfront.

sections.name: Как работает AI-конвейер
sections.purpose: Five phases (Brief → Research → Synthesizer → Stagehand → Downstream) shown as a typographic narrative, not abstract icons; link to /how-it-works for deep dive.

sections.name: Case studies
sections.purpose: Three to four delivery examples with hard metrics (hours to deploy, sections shipped) — anti-generic-testimonial.

sections.name: Тарифы
sections.purpose: Pricing summary with what is included and timeline per tier; link to /pricing for full spec-sheet.

sections.name: Команда
sections.purpose: Brief introduction: Tim + Victor + AI-pipeline as a named third member; link to /about.

sections.name: Заявка
sections.purpose: Final ask — brief / order form / direct Telegram contact.

sections.name: Контакты
sections.purpose: Telegram, email, working hours, geographic scope.

sections.name: Pipeline hero
sections.purpose: Honest framing — show the actual five phases instead of hiding behind 'magic AI' language.

sections.name: Phase 1 — Brief
sections.purpose: How a 30-minute Telegram intake produces structured brief.json; what we ask, what we never ask.

sections.name: Phase 2 — Research
sections.purpose: Niche-intelligence harvest: competitive landscape, visual tropes to avoid, underused directions.

sections.name: Phase 3 — Synthesizer
sections.purpose: Style-dimensions vector, color invariant, typography pair, sitemap, image and video briefs.

sections.name: Phase 4 — Stagehand
sections.purpose: Production-grade code: semantic HTML, accessibility, responsive on three viewports, deploy targets.

sections.name: Phase 5 — Downstream
sections.purpose: Image and video generation, copy polish, QA gates, deploy to Timeweb.

sections.name: Брифинг
sections.purpose: Convert from process trust into a concrete intake.

sections.name: Pricing hero
sections.purpose: Frame pricing as an honest spec-sheet, not 'starting from' marketing.

sections.name: Landing — 30 000 RUB / 24 ч
sections.purpose: One page, mobile adaptation, deploy. What is included and what is not.

sections.name: Multipage — 60 000 RUB / 36 ч
sections.purpose: Three to five pages, forms, analytics. Full inclusions list.

sections.name: Premium — 100 000 RUB / 48 ч
sections.purpose: Five-plus pages, separate mobile flow, custom animations, deeper copywriting.

sections.name: Что не входит
sections.purpose: Honest list of out-of-scope items — anti-hidden-fees signal.

sections.name: Pricing FAQ
sections.purpose: Common questions on timeline, ownership, hosting, revisions.

sections.name: Старт
sections.purpose: Tier-aware CTA into intake form.

sections.name: Case studies hero
sections.purpose: Frame: hours-to-deploy and concrete outcomes, not years-of-experience.

sections.name: Список кейсов
sections.purpose: Three to four cards with niche, tariff, hours-to-deploy, headline result.

sections.name: Свой кейс
sections.purpose: From observed proof into intake.

sections.name: Case hero
sections.purpose: Project name, niche, tariff, hours-to-deploy, headline metric.

sections.name: TL;DR
sections.purpose: Three to five bullet outcomes — measurable, not adjectival.

sections.name: Задача
sections.purpose: What the client came with; constraints; what they had tried before.

sections.name: Как делали
sections.purpose: Which pipeline phases mattered most; design choices and why.

sections.name: Результат
sections.purpose: Concrete metrics, screenshots, link to live site.

sections.name: Похожий проект
sections.purpose: Niche-similar CTA into intake.

sections.name: About hero
sections.purpose: Studio one-liner — two humans plus a named AI-pipeline, not a 'team of experts'.

sections.name: Tim и Victor
sections.purpose: Two founders, decomposed responsibility — who owns brief intake, who owns code, no anonymised 'we'.

sections.name: AI-конвейер как третий сотрудник
sections.purpose: Name the pipeline, list what it owns, show that it is supervised — anti-magical framing.

sections.name: Принципы
sections.purpose: Editorial restraint, no hype vocabulary, fixed timeline, transparent process.

sections.name: Поговорить
sections.purpose: Light CTA into Telegram intake.

sections.name: Contacts hero
sections.purpose: Direct ask — pick a channel, expected response time per channel.

sections.name: Форма заказа
sections.purpose: Structured intake — niche, tariff intent, deadline, link to current site if any.

sections.name: Прямой контакт
sections.purpose: Telegram @door2key, hello@belbes.ru, working hours GMT+3.

sections.name: Реквизиты
sections.purpose: Studio details and geographic scope (RU market).
Restrained error state — no jokes, single back-home CTA, link to sitemap.

image_briefs.prompt_seed: Editorial-restrained studio workspace shot: warm cream off-white surfaces (#F7F4EE), a single saturated red object (SBB Red #EB0000) as the only chromatic anchor — e.g. an open hardcover notebook with a crimson bookmark on a beech-wood desk, or a red ceramic cup beside a closed laptop. Diffused morning light from the left, soft long shadows. Subject centered, photographed straight-on at desk height. Generous negative space top-right for headline overlay. Mood: calm, technically-confident, anti-hype — no people in frame, no glowing AI orbs, no gradient backdrops. Photographic, magazine-editorial register, warm-neutral color grading.
image_briefs.negative_prompt: no neon gradients, no glowing orbs, no people, no AI/tech clichés, no centered text overlay in scene, no holograms, no abstract data visualizations

image_briefs.prompt_seed: Editorial duo portrait of two software-studio founders (one named Tim, the other Victor), both in their early-to-mid 30s, in a warm off-white studio interior (#F7F4EE walls). Casual smart attire — fine-knit sweaters or unstructured shirts, no suits, no hoodies. Relaxed but engaged expressions, half-smiles, eyes on camera. Natural diffused window light from the left, soft shadows. Medium shot, subjects offset slightly left in frame, generous negative space right for caption block. A single subtle red accent (SBB Red #EB0000) somewhere in scene — e.g. a red book spine on a low shelf behind them. Mood: trustworthy, technically-grounded, anti-corporate. Photographic, magazine-editorial register, warm-neutral color grading.
image_briefs.negative_prompt: no corporate suits, no stiff handshake poses, no fake bright laughter, no whiteboard with code behind them, no neon office, no AI-generated facial distortion artifacts

image_briefs.prompt_seed: Editorial product-style shot of a laptop or large monitor showing a finished website, photographed from a low three-quarter angle on a warm off-white surface (#F7F4EE). Screen content readable but not the focus. Single red object (#EB0000) in frame as chromatic anchor — pen, sticky note, book corner. Diffused side light, soft shadow. Magazine-editorial register. Composition leaves rule-of-thirds focal weight on the device. No people, no hands.
image_briefs.negative_prompt: no glowing screens, no neon RGB lighting, no busy desk clutter, no people, no centered logo overlay

video_briefs.treatment_rationale: Per brief.media_strategy.video_treatment: ambient motion is design intent (anchoring the editorial register with a quiet sign of life), but a real video render is overkill for this tier and worse on accessibility. Downstream merge-media with --css-animation-fallback will translate __VIDEO_hero_ambient__ into @keyframes + a styled <div class='anim-hero-ambient'> that respects prefers-reduced-motion.
video_briefs.prompt_seed: Very subtle parallax over the hero image — a slow 6-8 second drift of warm off-white light shifting across the surface, as if a cloud passes the window. Plus a barely-perceptible breathing scale (1.00 → 1.015 → 1.00) on the single red object. No camera moves on subject. Calm, almost-still.
video_briefs.css_fallback_intent: Two @keyframes layered: (1) `hero-light-drift` translateX(-2%) → translateX(2%) on a ::before pseudo overlay with linear-gradient warm-white over 14s ease-in-out infinite alternate; (2) `hero-red-breathe` scale(1) → scale(1.015) → scale(1) over 8s ease-in-out infinite on the red-accent element. Both wrapped in @media (prefers-reduced-motion: no-preference).

video_briefs.treatment_rationale: Decorative interstitial between major sections on /how-it-works (between Phase blocks). Same rationale as hero_ambient — design intent is a quiet motion cue marking phase boundaries, not a video render.
video_briefs.prompt_seed: On phase-boundary scroll into view: a thin horizontal hairline (#1F1B17) draws itself left-to-right over ~700ms, then a single red dot (#EB0000) travels along it from left edge to right edge over the following ~1.4s and stops at the right.
video_briefs.css_fallback_intent: Trigger on IntersectionObserver(0.4). @keyframes `hairline-draw` width 0 → 100% (700ms cubic-bezier(0.65,0,0.35,1)) on the line element; @keyframes `red-dot-travel` translateX(0) → translateX(calc(100% - 8px)) (1400ms 700ms ease-out) on the dot. Single play; not loopable. Wrapped in @media (prefers-reduced-motion: no-preference); reduced-motion variant just fades the elements in.

arbiter_reasoning: Phase-1 single-Synthesizer pass (trio + Arbiter deferred to Phase 4 per skill). Conservative MVP vector adjusted on two axes with explicit warrant: (1) era_reference shifted 60s-swiss-modernism → 2024-editorial because brief.tone_keywords explicitly include 'editorial' and niche_intel.aesthetic_lean names 'editorial-restrained tech-studio register' as the core direction — keeping the Swiss default would contradict the brief; (2) color_temperature shifted neutral → warm because brief.preferred_colors specifies #F7F4EE (warm off-white) as the intended surface, and niche_intel.competitive_visual_landscape.underused_directions calls out warm cream backdrop as the deliberate anti-dark-AI-norm contrast that BelBes wants to occupy. Density bumped 0.4 → 0.55 to accommodate long-form editorial home (5 phases as narrative, case studies with metrics) without flipping to dense; formality 0.85, decoration 0.05, energy 0.4 left at Phase-1 conservative defaults. Color invariant #EB0000 placed as primary per BelBes house-style; #1F1B17 from brief.preferred_colors used as text and as accent_secondary (anti-multi-accent — single saturated accent is the niche_intel-validated discipline). Typography pair Source Serif Pro display (editorial gravitas for headlines and process-step titles) + Inter body (restraint and readability) + JetBrains Mono (used to render pipeline phase names and prompt-as-content per niche_intel underused_directions about typography-led composition and pipeline-as-content). All three are open-source Google Fonts; no proprietary risk (PP Neue Montreal / GT Walsheim / Söhne deliberately not used despite editorial register, per skill house-style запрет). Composition field left empty per Phase-1 COMP-002 status; prompt-assembly will populate it when ≥12 components ship to the library. Sitemap is V6 shape (slug/role/viewport/sections, is_unique, is_template for case-study detail); 7 unique pages + 1 case_study template + 404, satisfying premium 7+ rule. Video_briefs intentionally populated with 2 entries despite the Phase-1 self-check default of empty, because (a) skill запрет explicitly states 'premium = добавить video_brief', (b) brief.media_strategy.min_video_roles=2 is operator-set, and (c) brief.media_strategy.video_treatment='css_animation_fallback' is honored — both briefs include a css_fallback_intent for downstream merge-media. Image_briefs include viewport_variants (3 viewports) and used_in_pages per V6.3.2 DATA-002; hero_main and founders_portrait both use subject_centered crop, case_thumb uses rule_of_thirds_focal.
